assertions.assertion: States that v4 uses HybridCache for token caching (not the old IDistributedCache directly)
assertions.rationale: Explicitly states: "Duende.AccessTokenManagement v4 uses HybridCache (introduced in ASP.NET Core 9) for token caching."

assertions.assertion: Explains the two-tier L1 (in-memory) + L2 (distributed) cache architecture
assertions.rationale: Explains L1 (in-memory) as fast process-local cache and L2 (distributed) as optional remote cache, with detailed flow.

assertions.assertion: Notes that explicit AddDistributedMemoryCache() registration is no longer required in v4
assertions.rationale: States: "There is no need to call AddDistributedMemoryCache() — this was required in v3 but is no longer necessary in v4."

assertions.assertion: Mentions CacheLifetimeBuffer as a configuration option for controlling token refresh timing
assertions.rationale: Dedicates a section to CacheLifetimeBuffer with code example showing `options.CacheLifetimeBuffer = 120;`.
